content_en:
The prayer chapter of Fath al-Mu'in is one of the most detailed in the work, reflecting the centrality of salah to daily Muslim life and the complexity of its rulings in the Shafi'i school. Al-Malibari's intermediate-level treatment gives students both the positions and enough explanation to apply them.

The chapter opens with the times of the five prayers, giving the beginning and end of each prayer's window with attention to the concepts of preferred time and permissible time. The fajr prayer, for example, has its most preferred time at the beginning of dawn (fajr sadiq) and extends until sunrise, but the most emphasized sunnah is to delay it somewhat — performing it when it is light enough to be certain of the prayer's validity. Al-Malibari addresses the common question of how to determine prayer times in locations where accurate instruments are unavailable, reflecting his awareness of the practical situation of his readers.

The seventeen pillars of Shafi'i prayer are covered with the detail appropriate for this level. On al-Fatiha, al-Malibari confirms the Shafi'i requirement of recitation in every raka'ah, including by the follower behind the imam. He explains the ruling on silent and audible prayers: in audible prayers, the follower waits for the imam to finish his recitation and then recites al-Fatiha quietly; in silent prayers, the follower recites at the same time as the imam. The sunnah of reciting the basmalah aloud in audible prayers is noted as a characteristic Shafi'i practice.

The chapter on congregation (jama'ah) is thorough on the conditions for following an imam. Al-Malibari addresses the question of following an imam of a different school — such as a Maliki imam in the Haramayn whose wudu was broken by cupping (a Maliki view) which does not break wudu in the Shafi'i school — and the Shafi'i principle that the validity of the prayer depends on the imam's actual state according to his own school, not the follower's assessment of it.

Fath al-Mu'in's coverage of the supererogatory prayers is particularly valuable. Al-Malibari covers the rawatib (confirmed sunnahs accompanying the five obligatory prayers), the duha prayer, the tahajjud and its virtues, and the night prayer in general. These rulings are practically important for students who seek to build a complete devotional life beyond the obligatory minimum.

The chapter on the Friday prayer addresses the conditions of its obligation and validity, the two khutbahs and their required elements, and the ruling on those who miss the jumu'ah and must perform zuhr instead.
content_id:
Bab salat dalam Fath al-Mu'in adalah salah satu yang paling terperinci dalam kitab ini, mencerminkan sentralitas salah dalam kehidupan Muslim sehari-hari dan kerumitan hukum-hukumnya dalam mazhab Syafi'i. Penyajian al-Malibari pada tingkat menengah memberikan kepada para pelajar baik posisi mazhab maupun penjelasan yang cukup untuk menerapkannya.

Bab ini dibuka dengan waktu-waktu shalat lima waktu, menyebutkan awal dan akhir setiap jendela waktu salat dengan memperhatikan konsep waktu utama dan waktu yang diperbolehkan. Salat Subuh misalnya memiliki waktu paling utama pada awal fajar (fajr shadiq) dan memanjang hingga terbit matahari, namun sunnah yang paling ditekankan adalah sedikit mengakhirkannya yakni melaksanakannya ketika sudah cukup terang untuk memastikan keabsahan salat. Al-Malibari membahas pertanyaan umum tentang cara menentukan waktu salat di tempat-tempat yang tidak tersedia alat ukur yang akurat, mencerminkan kesadarannya akan situasi praktis para pembacanya.

Tujuh belas rukun salat Syafi'i dibahas dengan detail yang sesuai untuk tingkat ini. Mengenai al-Fatihah, al-Malibari menegaskan keharusan mazhab Syafi'i untuk membacanya di setiap rakaat, termasuk oleh makmum di belakang imam. Beliau menjelaskan hukum salat sir dan jahr: dalam salat jahr, makmum menunggu imam selesai membaca lalu membaca al-Fatihah dengan pelan; dalam salat sir, makmum membaca bersamaan dengan imam. Sunnah membaca basmalah dengan keras dalam salat jahr dicatat sebagai praktik khas mazhab Syafi'i.

Bab tentang berjamaah sangat lengkap mengenai syarat-syarat mengikuti imam. Al-Malibari membahas masalah mengikuti imam dari mazhab berbeda seperti imam Maliki di Haramain yang wudunya batal karena bekam (menurut Maliki) sementara hal itu tidak membatalkan wudu dalam mazhab Syafi'i dan prinsip Syafi'i bahwa keabsahan salat bergantung pada keadaan imam yang sebenarnya menurut mazhabnya sendiri, bukan penilaian makmum.

Cakupan Fath al-Mu'in tentang salat sunnah sangat berharga. Al-Malibari membahas rawatib yaitu sunnah-sunnah yang menyertai salat lima waktu, salat Dhuha, tahajjud beserta keutamaannya, dan salat malam secara umum. Hukum-hukum ini penting secara praktis bagi para pelajar yang ingin membangun kehidupan ibadah yang lengkap melampaui kewajiban minimum.

Bab tentang salat Jumat membahas syarat-syarat kewajiban dan keabsahannya, dua khutbah dan rukun-rukunnya yang wajib, serta hukum bagi mereka yang melewatkan Jumat dan harus melaksanakan salat Zuhur sebagai gantinya.
content_ar: The الصلاة الباب of Fath al-Mu'in is one of the most detailed in the work, reflecting the centrality of الصلاة to daily مسلم الحياة and the complexity of its rulings in the الشافعي school. Al-Malibari's intermediate-level treatment gives students both the positions and enough explanation to apply them. The الباب opens with the times of the five الصلوات, giving the beginning and end of each الصلاة's window with attention to the concepts of preferred time and permissible time. The fajr الصلاة, على سبيل المثال, has its most preferred time at the beginning of dawn (fajr sadiq) and extends حتى sunrise, but the most emphasized السنة is to delay it somewhat — performing it عندما it is light enough to be certain of the الصلاة's validity. Al-Malibari addresses the common question of how to determine الصلاة times in locations where accurate instruments are unavailable, reflecting his awareness of the practical situation of his readers. The seventeen pillars of الشافعي الصلاة are covered with the detail appropriate for this level. On al-Fatiha, al-Malibari confirms the الشافعي requirement of التلاوة in every الركعة, بما في ذلك by the follower behind the الإمام. He explains the ruling on silent and audible الصلوات: in audible الصلوات, the follower waits for the الإمام to finish his التلاوة and then recites al-Fatiha quietly; in silent الصلوات, the follower recites at the same time as the الإمام. The السنة of reciting the basmalah aloud in audible الصلوات is noted as a characteristic الشافعي practice. The الباب on congregation (jama'ah) is thorough on the conditions for following an الإمام. Al-Malibari addresses the question of following an الإمام of a different school — مثل a المالكي الإمام in the Haramayn whose الوضوء was broken by cupping (a المالكي view) which does not break الوضوء in the الشافعي school — and the الشافعي principle that the validity of the الصلاة depends on the الإمام's actual state وفقاً لـ his own school, not the follower's assessment of it. Fath al-Mu'in's coverage of the supererogatory الصلوات is particularly valuable. Al-Malibari covers the rawatib (confirmed sunnahs accompanying the five obligatory الصلوات), the duha الصلاة, the tahajjud and its الفضائل, and the night الصلاة in general. These rulings are practically المهم for students who seek to build a complete devotional الحياة beyond the obligatory minimum. The الباب on the Friday الصلاة addresses the conditions of its الالتزام and validity, the two khutbahs and their required elements, and the ruling on those who miss the الجمعة and must perform zuhr instead.
